result.markdown:
---
title: Data Wrangling II
format: stat198slides-revealjs
---

## Agenda

Two big ideas today:

1. Data Pipelines
2. Grouped Operations

:::{.notes}
Before class, sketch out the effect that the different dplyr verbs from last class have on the shape of a data frame.
:::

# Data pipelines

##

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(dplyr)
star_wars <- data.frame(
    name = c("Anakin", "Padme", "Luke", "JarJar"),
    homeworld = c("Tatooine", "Naboo", "Tatooine", "Naboo"),
    height = c(1.8, 1.6, 1.7, 1.9),
    weight = c(84, 45, 77, 90)
)
```
:::


Using base R data frame subsetting, create from `star_wars`...

::: nonincremental
4. [The average height of characters from Tatooine]{style="color: red;"}
:::

. . .

> Let's look at three ways to solve this.


## Nesting {auto-animate=true}


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
summarize(filter(star_wars, homeworld == "Tatooine"), mean(height))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
  mean(height)
1         1.75
```


:::
:::


. . .

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
- Must be read from the inside out 👎
- Hard to keep track of arguments 👎
:::
::: {.column}
- All in one line of code 👍
- Only refer to one data frame 👍
:::
:::

## Step-by-step {auto-animate=true}


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars2 <- filter(star_wars, homeworld == "Tatooine")
summarize(star_wars2, mean(height))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
  mean(height)
1         1.75
```


:::
:::


. . .

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
- Have to repeaet data frame names 👎
- Creates unnecessary objects 👎
:::
::: {.column}
- Stores intermediate objects 👍
- Can be read from top to bottom 👍
:::
:::

## {background-image="images/pipeline.png" background-size="contain"}

## Data Pipelines in R

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
![](images/magrittr-hex.png)

2016: `magrittr` introduces `%>%` 
:::
::: {.column .fragment}
[`|>`]{style="font-size:3em;"}

2021:

> *R now provides a simple native forward pipe syntax |>. The simple form of the forward pipe inserts the left-hand side as the first argument in the right-hand side call.*
:::
:::


## Using the Pipe Operator {auto-animate=true}


::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
```
:::



## Using the Pipe Operator {auto-animate=true}


::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    filter(homeworld == "Tatooine") |>
```
:::



## Using the Pipe Operator {auto-animate=true}


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    filter(homeworld == "Tatooine") |>
    summarize(mean(height))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
  mean(height)
1         1.75
```


:::
:::


. . .

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
- 🤷‍♂️
:::
::: {.column}
- Can be read like an english paragraph 👍
- Only type the data once 👍
- No leftover objects 👍
:::
:::


## Understanding your pipeline

It's good practice to understand the output of each line by *breaking the pipe*.

. . .

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    select(homeworld) |>
    filter(mean(height))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-error}

```
Error in `filter()`:
ℹ In argument: `mean(height)`.
Caused by error:
! object 'height' not found
```


:::
:::

:::
::: {.column .fragment}


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    select(homeworld)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
  homeworld
1  Tatooine
2     Naboo
3  Tatooine
4     Naboo
```


:::
:::

:::
:::


## 


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |> # A #<<
    filter(homeworld == "Naboo",
           name %in% c("Padme", "JarJar")) |> # B #<<
    select(height, weight) |> # C #<<
    summarize(mean(height), sd(height),
              mean(weight), sd(weight)) # D #<<
```
:::


**Question:** What are the dimensions of the data frame at each stage of the pipe: A, B, C, and D?

{{< countdown "2:00" >}}

::: notes
Show answer in drop by breaking the pipe
:::

## `|>` works everywhere

> *R now provides a simple native forward pipe syntax |>. The simple form of the forward pipe inserts the left-hand side as the first argument in the right-hand side call.*

. . .


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
factor(c("cat", "cat", "dog")) |> summary()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
cat dog 
  2   1 
```


:::
:::



# Grouped Operations

##

##

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(dplyr)
star_wars <- data.frame(
    name = c("Anakin", "Padme", "Luke", "JarJar"),
    homeworld = c("Tatooine", "Naboo", "Tatooine", "Naboo"),
    height = c(1.8, 1.6, 1.7, 1.9),
    weight = c(84, 45, 77, 90)
)
```
:::


Using base R data frame subsetting, create from `star_wars`...

4. The average height of characters [from Tatooine]{.fragment .strike} [across each planet]{.fragment .fade-up}

. . .

> *You could set up two pipelines with different filters. But there's a better way.*

## `group_by()` {auto-animate=true}

Flag the rows of a dataframe as belong to a group defined by a factor. For use in downstream operations.

. . .


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    group_by(homeworld)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 4 × 4
# Groups:   homeworld [2]
  name   homeworld height weight
  <chr>  <chr>      <dbl>  <dbl>
1 Anakin Tatooine     1.8     84
2 Padme  Naboo        1.6     45
3 Luke   Tatooine     1.7     77
4 JarJar Naboo        1.9     90
```


:::
:::


## `group_by()` {auto-animate=true}

Flag the rows of a dataframe as belong to a group defined by a factor. For use in downstream operations.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    group_by(homeworld) |>
    summarize(mean(height))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 2 × 2
  homeworld `mean(height)`
  <chr>              <dbl>
1 Naboo               1.75
2 Tatooine            1.75
```


:::
:::



## 

Draw diagram


## `group_by()` with `summarize()` {auto-animate=true}

. . .


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    summarize(mean(height))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
  mean(height)
1         1.75
```


:::
:::



## `group_by()` with `summarize()` {auto-animate=true}


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    group_by(homeworld) |>
    summarize(mean(height))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 2 × 2
  homeworld `mean(height)`
  <chr>              <dbl>
1 Naboo               1.75
2 Tatooine            1.75
```


:::
:::


. . .

> Makes a summary row for each group.


## `group_by()` with `filter()` {auto-animate=true}

. . .


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    filter(height == max(height))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::



## `group_by()` with `filter()` {auto-animate=true}


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    group_by(homeworld) |>
    filter(height == max(height))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 2 × 4
# Groups:   homeworld [2]
  name   homeworld height weight
  <chr>  <chr>      <dbl>  <dbl>
1 Anakin Tatooine     1.8     84
2 JarJar Naboo        1.9     90
```


:::
:::


. . .

> Changes the scope of functions inside `filter()` to operate within groups.

::: notes
Can bring up drop to demonstrate how a condition like height > 1.7 will operate the same with or without the grouping.
:::


## `group_by()` with `arrange()` {auto-animate=true}

. . .


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    arrange(desc(height))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
2 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
```


:::
:::



## `group_by()` with `arrange()` {auto-animate=true}


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    group_by(homeworld) |>
    arrange(desc(height))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 4 × 4
# Groups:   homeworld [2]
  name   homeworld height weight
  <chr>  <chr>      <dbl>  <dbl>
1 JarJar Naboo        1.9     90
2 Anakin Tatooine     1.8     84
3 Luke   Tatooine     1.7     77
4 Padme  Naboo        1.6     45
```


:::
:::


. . .

> Arrange ignores `group_by()` and is always global

Why???


##


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    arrange(homeworld, height)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
2 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
```


:::
:::



## `group_by()` with `mutate()` {auto-animate=true}

. . .


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    mutate(height_z = (height - mean(height)) / sd(height))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight   height_z
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84  0.3872983
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45 -1.1618950
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77 -0.3872983
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90  1.1618950
```


:::
:::



## `group_by()` with `mutate()` {auto-animate=true}


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    group_by(homeworld) |>
    mutate(height_z = (height - mean(height)) / sd(height))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 4 × 5
# Groups:   homeworld [2]
  name   homeworld height weight height_z
  <chr>  <chr>      <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>
1 Anakin Tatooine     1.8     84    0.707
2 Padme  Naboo        1.6     45   -0.707
3 Luke   Tatooine     1.7     77   -0.707
4 JarJar Naboo        1.9     90    0.707
```


:::
:::


. . .

> Changes the scope of functions inside `mutate()` to operate within groups.


## Statefulness

What will this produce?

. . .


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    group_by(homeworld) |>
    mutate(height_z = (height - mean(height)) / sd(height)) |>
    summarize(mean(height_z))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 2 × 2
  homeworld `mean(height_z)`
  <chr>                <dbl>
1 Naboo                    0
2 Tatooine                 0
```


:::
:::


. . .

> Once grouped, a data frame stays grouped until reduced to one-row-per group or it is ungrouped.

::: notes
can break the pipe after mutate to show it is still grouped
:::

## Statefulness

What will this produce?


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    group_by(homeworld) |>
    mutate(height_z = (height - mean(height)) / sd(height)) |>
    ungroup() |>
    summarize(mean(height_z))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 1 × 1
  `mean(height_z)`
             <dbl>
1                0
```


:::
:::



## Shortcut: `.by`

Most `dplyr` functions have a `.by` argument to apply the function to the groups of another variable.

- Not stateful
- Good for single line operations

. . .


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars |>
    summarize(mean(height), .by = homeworld)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
  homeworld mean(height)
1  Tatooine         1.75
2     Naboo         1.75
```


:::
:::




<!-- # Tidy Data

## Tidy Data Frame as a Statistical Concept

:::{.columns}

:::{.column width=50%}
![](images/tidy-data.png)
:::

:::{.column width=50%}
In tidy data:

1. Each *variable* forms a column.
2. Each *observation* forms a row.
3. Each type of *observational unit* forms a table.

Note each *cell* contains a value.
:::
::: -->

include-after-body:
<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
